conflicting_values: 143 different shades (official US IGORA ROYAL page, fetched June 2026)
133 distinct code/name entries extractable from the (c) 2021 US Paper Swatch Chart
source_conflicts.reason: The 2021 chart text layer yields 133 entries (incl. E-1, Pastelfier and 4 named Silver Whites); the current official page claims 143. Some chart swatches/codes may be lost in text extraction or the portfolio may have been extended after 2021. Unresolved.

conflicting_values: 76 distinct codes enumerated in the 2021 booklet portfolio overview
IFU additionally references '8 9.5-Toners, 5 NEW 10- Toners and a CLEAR 0-00' (the five 10- toner codes are not enumerated in any fetched source)
source_conflicts.preferred_value: 76 codes recorded (all individually observed in the booklet)
source_conflicts.reason: Only individually observed codes were recorded. The five 10- toners exist per the 2022 IFU and the official page (Level 10 Toner longevity claim) but their codes were never printed in fetched sources, so they are logged as missing rather than invented.

conflicting_values: '04NB -> 4-64' printed once in the booklet's Redken Shades EQ conversion table
4-64 absent from the booklet portfolio overview (which lists 4-6, 4-63, 4-46)
source_conflicts.reason: Appears only once, in a conversion table; likely a misprint of 4-63 or 4-46. Not recorded as a shade record.

conflicting_values: '5-75' appears in the text extraction of the booklet swatch page 5
portfolio overview lists 5-7 and 5-65 but no 5-75
source_conflicts.preferred_value: treated as a text-extraction kerning artifact of adjacent codes 5-7 / 5-65
source_conflicts.reason: Page 5 extraction shows several kerning-split codes (e.g. '7-4 8' for 7-48, '9.5-4 9' for 9.5-49); 5-75 is not corroborated anywhere else. Not recorded as a shade.

issue_log: IGORA ROYAL: swatch-grid family/column assignment (Naturals, Cendres & Cools, Beiges & Golds, Chocolates, Reds, Absolutes, Highlifts & Pastels, Specialties) is not recoverable from the PDF text layer; sub-range was assigned only via printed series prefixes (10-, 12-, 9.5-, L-, 0-, three-digit Absolutes codes, Silver Whites names).

IGORA ROYAL: chart legend marks shades also available as IGORA COLOR10 or IGORA VIBRANCE using graphic symbols that are lost in text extraction; per-shade cross-line availability could not be recorded, and a small number of listed codes could in principle belong to those companion lines on the combined chart.
IGORA ROYAL: the chart's '100% coverage shades' helpful hint extracts garbled as '-0 -0 -1 -4 -63' (depth digits missing); recorded verbatim in the line technical record with a gap note.
IGORA ROYAL: SILVER WHITES shades (Silver, Slate Grey, Grey Lilac, Dove Grey) and Pastelfier are printed as names only in the text layer; numeric codes (if printed on the chart) were not extractable.
IGORA VIBRANCE: the five '10- Toners' referenced by the 2022 IFU and the official US page (Level 10 Toner, up to 6 shampoos) are not enumerated by code in any fetched source; codes missing.
IGORA VIBRANCE: booklet portfolio pages merge family columns in text extraction; shade families were instead derived from the IFU's printed tone-family grouping.
Both lines: no per-shade names are printed in the fetched sources (except ROYAL Silver Whites names); shade_name is null throughout.

Both lines: official swatch chart shows swatches for ROYAL shades (official_swatch_image_exists=true based on the document being a paper swatch chart); VIBRANCE swatch presence left null because the booklet's swatch page could not be reliably matched code-to-swatch.
